brands.name: SkyRun Vacation Rentals
brands.notes: Vacation rental property management franchise that started as a single Keystone, CO operation (brand operating since early 2000s) and now has ~50 locally owned locations managing 1,300+ homes across the US and Canada. Low-overhead, home-based model; franchisor publishes no earnings claims.

riskFlags.title: Emerging franchise system with short franchising track record
riskFlags.detail: Although the brand dates to 2004, Entrepreneur lists SkyRun as franchising only since 2022, with ~50 units; limited history operating as a franchisor.

riskFlags.title: No published earnings claims; results depend heavily on local market
riskFlags.detail: SkyRun's own franchising page states 'We cannot put an exact number on how much you will make' — profitability depends on market size, property mix, staffing and pricing decisions of each owner.

franchiseFee.note: Initial franchise fee range $55,000-$75,000 depending on territory; lower bound shown.

totalUnits.note: Franchisor markets '40+ independently owned locations' managing 1,300+ homes (US and Canada).

yearFounded.note: Company PR also describes a 100-property start at Keystone Resort, CO around 2002.

item19Available.note: Franchisor publishes no earnings claims ('We cannot put an exact number on how much you will make').

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows strong recent unit growth and Top New & Emerging Franchises recognition (2024, 2025).

ownerTypes.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (absentee ownership and part-time operation allowed)

brands.name: Slice House by Tony Gemignani
brands.notes: Fast-casual NY/Sicilian-style pizza franchise from 13-time World Pizza Champion Tony Gemignani; ~23-30 restaurants and stadium kiosks open (concentrated in CA/NV/ID plus arena kiosks) with a large sold-but-unopened pipeline. Note: slicehousepizza.com is an unrelated restaurant; the brand site is slicehouse.com.

riskFlags.title: Large sold pipeline vs. few open units
riskFlags.detail: Roughly 45 franchise units plus ~24 stadium/arena licensed locations reported open or in development, while only ~23-30 locations are actually open — execution risk typical of fast-selling emerging brands.
riskFlags.source: FoodFranchise.com buyer's guide / Slice House franchise site

riskFlags.title: No exclusive territory protection per FDD-derived summary
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived analysis notes franchisees receive only a site-selection territory and 'may face direct competition from other franchisees, company outlets' (Entrepreneur's listing conflicts, showing exclusive territory available — verify in current FDD).

riskFlags.title: Young franchise system tied to a celebrity chef brand
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since ~2021-2022; system value is closely tied to Tony Gemignani's personal brand and ongoing involvement.

statesOpen.note: Restaurants operating; brand home market (San Francisco)
Restaurants operating per franchisor site
Restaurants operating per franchisor site; brand states readiness to expand across the country (plus stadium kiosks nationwide)

royaltyPercent.note: Some third-party FDD summaries list 6%; Entrepreneur's current listing shows 5%.

adFundPercent.note: Ad royalty listed as 1%-3% range; lower bound shown. Plus $50/week technology fee.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor site states minimum liquid capital $250,000; Entrepreneur lists $150,000.

totalUnits.note: Franchisor site claims ~30 including stadium/arena kiosks.

yearFounded.note: Original Slice House opened 2010; the franchise concept was packaged ~2022.

trainingWeeks.note: Approximation from 250 hours of on-the-job training plus classroom training listed by Entrepreneur.

isGrowing.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (ranked #44 Top New & Emerging Franchises 2026)

brands.name: Smalls Sliders
brands.notes: Drive-thru/walk-up cheeseburger-slider QSR built from shipping-container-style modular 'Cans'; founded in Baton Rouge by Walk-On's founder Brandon Landry and backed by 10 Point Capital and Drew Brees. One of the fastest-selling emerging QSR franchises with 350+ units open or under development but only a few dozen actually open.

riskFlags.title: CEO turnover during hypergrowth
riskFlags.detail: CEO Maria Rivera exited in early 2025 after about two years; former Tropical Smoothie Cafe CEO Charles Watson took over in April 2025, followed by a broader executive team rebuild.

riskFlags.title: Very large sold pipeline vs. small open base, with high unit investment
riskFlags.detail: 350+ Cans open or under development across ~30 states versus only ~21 open units at year-end 2024 (Franchise Times) / ~32 per Entrepreneur 2026; franchisees commit $1.3M-$2.0M per unit into a system with a short operating track record.

riskFlags.title: Institutional backing and rapid systemwide sales growth
riskFlags.detail: Backed by 10 Point Capital with Drew Brees as investor; systemwide sales $32.8M in 2024, up 102.5% year over year; named fastest-growing brand in the 2026 Datassential 500.

nationwide.source: franchising.com Smalls Sliders listing (all 50 states shown as available markets)
nationwide.note: All 50 U.S. states listed as available franchise markets; ~30 states already have units open or in development and some initial development phases reported sold out.

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (corroborated by Franchise Times)
franchiseFee.note: $35,000 first unit; some sources list $30,000 for additional units under development agreements.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (corroborated by Franchise Times Top 400)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (corroborated by Franchise Times Top 400)

adFundPercent.note: Plus a minimum grand-opening marketing spend reported around $15,000.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Net worth requirement $4,000,000 — brand targets well-capitalized multi-unit developers.

totalUnits.note: Franchise Times Top 400 reported 21 units (19 franchised, 2 company-owned) at year-end 2024; 350+ open or under development per franchisor.

yearFounded.note: First Baton Rouge location; Entrepreneur lists founding 2018.

yearFranchising.note: franchising.com lists franchising since 2023; first franchised Cans opened 2023.

multiUnitAvailable.source: Smalls Sliders press release (200 franchise units sold via multi-unit development agreements)

fddAvailable.source: Smalls Sliders FDD issued 5/8/2025 (restfinance.com public copy)

isGrowing.source: Franchise Times Top 400 (2025): sales +102.5%, units +50% YoY; 2026 Datassential 500 fastest-growing brand

brands.name: SnapHouss
brands.notes: Home-based real estate photography/videography franchise founded in Canada in 2020, franchising since 2021, now marketing across North America. Low entry cost but a very young system; FDD-derived unit and revenue figures are far smaller than the brand's marketing claims.

riskFlags.title: Marketing claims diverge sharply from FDD-derived unit count
riskFlags.detail: Brand markets '78+ locations serving 200+ towns and cities across North America' while its 2024 FDD-derived data showed only 11 franchised US units (0 company-owned); Entrepreneur 2026 lists 80 units. Verify what counts as a 'location' before relying on growth claims.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback FDD summary vs. SnapHouss franchise page

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 earnings claim; third-party revenue estimates are very low
riskFlags.detail: SnapHouss does not disclose average unit revenue in its FDD; third-party estimates of average gross revenue per unit run roughly $83K-$102K, modest against a 7% royalty plus 4% ad fee.

riskFlags.title: Very new system with wide, tier-based franchise fee
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2020, franchising since 2021; the initial franchise fee spans $9,900 to $99,000 depending on territory size, so per-deal economics vary widely.

franchiseFee.note: Tiered by territory size: $9,900-$99,000; lower bound (smallest territory) shown.

totalInvestmentMax.note: Other FDD summaries list up to ~$130,550.

totalUnits.note: 2024 FDD-derived data showed 11 franchised US units; brand counts North America-wide 'locations' — see riskFlags.

yearFounded.note: Founded in Canada; US franchising entity HQ reported in Las Vegas, NV.

canRunPartTime.note: Entrepreneur lists part-time operation 'No' although brand marketing mentions part-time options.

exclusiveTerritories.note: FDD summary describes protected territory of minimum 50,000 dwellings per zip-code group.

veteranDiscount.note: Grand-opening advertising fee ($5,500) waived for veterans purchasing large territories.

item19Available.note: 'Snaphouss does not disclose the average unit volume (average revenue) in its FDD.'

isGrowing.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory (+515% unit growth shown)

brands.name: Snapchef
brands.notes: On-demand culinary staffing and training company founded 2002 in Massachusetts (nearly 1 million shifts filled for 700+ organizations) that began franchising in 2022; the franchise system itself is tiny (~4-6 units), concentrated in New England.

riskFlags.title: Very small, very new franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Although the parent staffing business dates to 2002, franchising began only in 2022 and the system has roughly 4-6 units, giving prospective franchisees a minimal validation pool.

riskFlags.title: Geographic concentration in Massachusetts/New England
riskFlags.detail: Headquartered in Holden/Boston, MA with existing operations concentrated in New England; performance of the model outside its home region is unproven.

statesOpen.note: Brand offers franchises nationwide except 13 registration states it lists as unavailable (CA, HI, IL, IN, MD, MN, NY, ND, RI, SD, VA, WA, WI)
